motivation.quote: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
motivation.author: Lao Tzu

motivation.quote: It does not matter how slowly you go as long as you do not stop.
motivation.author: Confucius

motivation.quote: Well begun is half done.
motivation.author: Aristotle

motivation.quote: Fortune favors the bold.
motivation.author: Virgil

motivation.quote: Great works are performed not by strength, but by perseverance.
motivation.author: Samuel Johnson

motivation.quote: Energy and persistence conquer all things.
motivation.author: Benjamin Franklin

motivation.quote: Nothing is impossible to a willing heart.
motivation.author: John Heywood

motivation.quote: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
motivation.author: Mark Twain

motivation.quote: A man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
motivation.author: Confucius

motivation.quote: Great oaks from little acorns grow.
motivation.author: Proverb

motivation.quote: The harder the conflict, the greater the triumph.
motivation.author: George Washington

motivation.quote: A smooth sea never made a skilled sailor.
motivation.author: Proverb

motivation.quote: Our greatest glory is not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
motivation.author: Confucius

motivation.quote: Where there is a will, there is a way.
motivation.author: Proverb

motivation.quote: Rome was not built in a day.
motivation.author: Proverb

motivation.quote: Slow and steady wins the race.
motivation.author: Aesop

motivation.quote: Keep your face always toward the sunshine—and shadows will fall behind you.
motivation.author: Walt Whitman

motivation.quote: What we think, we become.
motivation.author: Buddha

motivation.quote: No great thing is created suddenly.
motivation.author: Epictetus

motivation.quote: Things won are done; joy's soul lies in the doing.
motivation.author: William Shakespeare

motivation.quote: No pressure, no diamonds.
motivation.author: Thomas Carlyle

motivation.quote: Work conquers all things.
motivation.author: Virgil

motivation.quote: Success is the child of audacity.
motivation.author: Benjamin Disraeli

motivation.quote: Our doubts are traitors, and make us lose the good we oft might win, by fearing to attempt.
motivation.author: William Shakespeare

motivation.quote: Our remedies oft in ourselves do lie.
motivation.author: William Shakespeare

success.quote: By failing to prepare, you are preparing to fail.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: I am a slow walker, but I never walk back.
success.author: Abraham Lincoln

success.quote: There are no gains without pains.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: Success usually comes to those who are too busy to be looking for it.
success.author: Henry David Thoreau

success.quote: The heights by great men reached and kept were not attained by sudden flight, but they, while their companions slept, were toiling upward in the night.
success.author: Henry Wadsworth Longfellow

success.quote: Success is the sum of small efforts, repeated day in and day out.
success.author: Robert Collier

success.quote: He who would leap high must take a long run.
success.author: Proverb

success.quote: Nothing great was ever achieved without enthusiasm.
success.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

success.quote: Well done is better than well said.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: The secret of success is constancy to purpose.
success.author: Benjamin Disraeli

success.quote: Industry, perseverance and frugality make fortune.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: Early to bed and early to rise makes a man healthy, wealthy, and wise.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: A penny saved is a penny earned.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: Diligence is the mother of good luck.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: He that can have patience can have what he will.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: Lost time is never found again.
success.author: Benjamin Franklin

success.quote: A person who is master of patience is master of everything else.
success.author: George Savile

success.quote: No man is born into the world whose work is not born with him.
success.author: James Russell Lowell

success.quote: Luck is what happens when preparation meets opportunity.
success.author: Seneca

success.quote: All things come to those who wait.
success.author: Proverb

success.quote: The early bird catches the worm.
success.author: Proverb

success.quote: Practice makes perfect.
success.author: Proverb

success.quote: A stitch in time saves nine.
success.author: Proverb

success.quote: Nothing ventured, nothing gained.
success.author: Proverb

success.quote: No pain, no gain.
success.author: Proverb

life.quote: A life unexamined is not worth living.
life.author: Socrates

life.quote: Happiness depends upon ourselves.
life.author: Aristotle

life.quote: To thine own self be true.
life.author: William Shakespeare

life.quote: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
life.author: Aristotle

life.quote: You have power over your mind, not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
life.author: Marcus Aurelius

life.quote: The happiness of your life depends upon the quality of your thoughts.
life.author: Marcus Aurelius

life.quote: No man is free who is not master of himself.
life.author: Epictetus

life.quote: What lies behind us and what lies before us are tiny matters compared with what lies within us.
life.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

life.quote: To be yourself in a world that is constantly trying to make you something else is the greatest accomplishment.
life.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

life.quote: The greatest wealth is to live content with little.
life.author: Plato

life.quote: To live is the rarest thing in the world. Most people exist, that is all.
life.author: Oscar Wilde

life.quote: Life is short, and truth works far and lives long; let us speak the truth.
life.author: Arthur Schopenhauer

life.quote: The mind once enlightened cannot again become dark.
life.author: Thomas Paine

life.quote: Kindness is the language which the deaf can hear and the blind can see.
life.author: Mark Twain

life.quote: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
life.author: Seneca

life.quote: The best portion of a good man's life is his little, nameless, unremembered acts of kindness and of love.
life.author: William Wordsworth

life.quote: All life is an experiment. The more experiments you make the better.
life.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

life.quote: In the end, it is not the years in your life that count. It is the life in your years.
life.author: Abraham Lincoln

life.quote: I like the dreams of the future better than the history of the past.
life.author: Thomas Jefferson

life.quote: The world is full of magical things patiently waiting for our wits to grow sharper.
life.author: Bertrand Russell

life.quote: The years teach much which the days never know.
life.author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

life.quote: A friend is one soul abiding in two bodies.
life.author: Aristotle

life.quote: The greatest glory in living lies not in never falling, but in rising every time we fall.
life.author: Confucius

life.quote: Every cloud has a silver lining.
life.author: Proverb

life.quote: Live and let live.
life.author: Proverb

study.quote: Learning never exhausts the mind.
study.author: Leonardo da Vinci

study.quote: The roots of education are bitter, but the fruit is sweet.
study.author: Aristotle

study.quote: An investment in knowledge pays the best interest.
study.author: Benjamin Franklin

study.quote: The more I read, the more I acquire, the more certain I am that I know nothing.
study.author: Voltaire

study.quote: Reading is to the mind what exercise is to the body.
study.author: Joseph Addison

study.quote: The man who does not read has no advantage over the man who cannot read.
study.author: Mark Twain

study.quote: A room without books is like a body without a soul.
study.author: Marcus Tullius Cicero

study.quote: Learning without thought is labor lost; thought without learning is perilous.
study.author: Confucius

study.quote: Knowledge is power.
study.author: Francis Bacon

study.quote: The only true wisdom is in knowing you know nothing.
study.author: Socrates

study.quote: What we learn with pleasure we never forget.
study.author: Alfred Mercier

study.quote: Read not to contradict and confute, nor to believe and take for granted, nor to find talk and discourse, but to weigh and consider.
study.author: Francis Bacon

study.quote: Knowledge comes, but wisdom lingers.
study.author: Alfred Lord Tennyson

study.quote: Tell me and I forget. Teach me and I remember. Involve me and I learn.
study.author: Benjamin Franklin

study.quote: He who opens a school door, closes a prison.
study.author: Victor Hugo

study.quote: Study the past if you would define the future.
study.author: Confucius

study.quote: To know, is to know that you know nothing. That is the meaning of true knowledge.
study.author: Socrates

study.quote: Knowledge is of two kinds. We know a subject ourselves, or we know where we can find information upon it.
study.author: Samuel Johnson

study.quote: A little learning is a dangerous thing.
study.author: Alexander Pope

study.quote: The more you know, the more you know you don't know.
study.author: Proverb

study.quote: A wise man learns more from his enemies than a fool from his friends.
study.author: Proverb

study.quote: A book is like a garden carried in the pocket.
study.author: Chinese Proverb

study.quote: A wise man will make more opportunities than he finds.
study.author: Francis Bacon

study.quote: The pen is mightier than the sword.
study.author: Edward Bulwer-Lytton

study.quote: The wise learn many things from their enemies.
study.author: Aristophanes
